nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
## Read Me

This workflow allows you to do account research with the web using AI.

The advanced AI module has 2 capabilities: 
- Research Google using SerpAPI
- Visit and get website content using a sub-workflow


From an unstructured input like a domain or a company name. 

It will return the following properties: 
- domain
- company Linkedin Url
- cheapest plan
- has free trial
- has entreprise plan
- has API
- market (B2B or B2C)


The strength of n8n here is that you can adapt this workflow to research whatever information you need.

You just have to precise it in the prompt and to precise the output format in the "Strutured Output Parser" module.

[Click here to find more detailed instructions with video guide.]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

nodes.name: AI company researcher

parameters.text:
=This is the company I want you to research info about:
{{ $json.company_input }}

Return me:
- the linkedin URL of the company
- the domain of the company. in this format ([domain].[tld])
- market: if they are B2B or B2C. Only reply by "B2B" or "B2B"
- the lowest paid plan the company is offering. If you are not sure, reply null.
- the latest case study URL published on the website (find case study hub using google, and return the first case study link)
- tell me if the company offer an API
- tell me if the company has an enterprise plan
- tell me if the company has a free trial mentionned in their homepage. reply false if you don't find strong evidence.
- return an array with up to 5 tools the company is integrated with

nodes.notes: This agent node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

3. Add the URL to this node and the node **"Google Sheets - Update Row with data"**

parameters.content:
## Read Me

This workflow allows you to do account research with the web using AI.

The advanced AI module has 2 capabilities: 
- Research Google using SerpAPI
- Visit and get website content using a sub-workflow


From an unstructured input like a domain or a company name. 

It will return the following properties: 
- domain
- company Linkedin Url
- cheapest plan
- has free trial
- has entreprise plan
- has API
- market (B2B or B2C)


The strength of n8n here is that you can adapt this workflow to research whatever information you need.

You just have to precise it in the prompt and to precise the output format in the "Strutured Output Parser" module.

[Click here to find more detailed instructions with video guide.]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

### Process rows 1 by 1
This module will allow us to process rows 1 by 1
Precise here the format in which you need the data to be
### Ask AI what are the information you are looking for about the company
Get your free API key here {{ $env.API_BASE_URL }}
Run the workflow manually or activate it to run it every 2 hours

Instead of SERP API module, you can also use this custom module for ScrapingBee. It is more cost-efficient.

Get your free API key here {{ $env.API_BASE_URL }}

In this workflow, I use Google Sheets to store the results. 

You can use my template to get started faster:

1. [Click on this link to get the template]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
2. Make a copy of the Sheets
3. Add the URL to this node and the node **"Google Sheets - Update Row with data"**

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
google Sheets  O Auth2  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking "Test workflow"".
Open the scheduler node "Schedule Trigger".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get website content" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "SerpAPI - Search Google" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Structured Output Parser" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Loop Over Items" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Sheets - Update Row with data" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Merge data" node (n8n-nodes-base.merge) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get rows to enrich" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "AI company researcher" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Search Google with ScrapingBee" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note6" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note7" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Input".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "AI Researcher Output Data".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
